title: Front-half season continuity phase
summary: After beach joins the field-season board and the front-half station language warms up, lane 1 should spend its next pass on continuity. This wave tightens coast-facing season wording across board, journal, and map seams, then adds one compact front-half stop-point or recap seam so the early season feels as intentional as the inland half.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the front-half season continuity handoff.
execution_order.why_now: Beach and scrub can join the season board, but the early-game continuity still needs a stronger shared language.
The front half should feel like one chapter across board, journal, and map seams.
Continuity work can drift into recap clutter if not checked.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a front-half stop-point or recap handoff.
execution_order.why_now: Once continuity improves, the early loop also needs a calmer place to pause.

execution_order.goal: Implement a front-half stop-point or recap seam.
execution_order.why_now: The coast-facing loop should feel as complete and pausable as the inland half.

execution_order.goal: Review the front-half stop-point or recap seam.
execution_order.why_now: The routes shell should stay calm rather than growing denser.

guardrails: Reuse existing board, atlas, journal-card, and map-footer seams.
Do not add another station page, recap panel, or navigation HUD.
Prefer one shared phrase family over multiple new labels.
Keep the early loop routes-first and notebook-toned.
